articles.headline: Micron Technology Delivers Blockbuster Fiscal Q3 Results Driven by AI Memory Crunch
articles.summary: Micron's revenue quadrupled year-over-year to $41.46 billion with net profit jumping to $28.2 billion, signaling massive demand for AI infrastructure memory. This surge is forcing suppliers to secure tight hardware supplies and may lead to increased memory costs for businesses deploying AI systems.

articles.headline: SpaceX and OpenAI Startup Reflection AI Sign $6.3 Billion Infrastructure Deal, Altering AI Chip Access
articles.summary: SpaceX and Reflection AI have secured a $6.3 billion multi-year computing infrastructure agreement, granting Reflection AI access to high-powered Nvidia GB300 chips housed in SpaceX's Colossus 2 data center. This deal significantly impacts AI infrastructure costs and chip availability, potentially reshaping compute strategies for businesses reliant on Nvidia hardware.

articles.headline: Oracle Lays Off 21,000 Employees in Aggressive Pivot Toward AI and Cloud Infrastructure
articles.summary: Oracle has terminated 21,000 workers as part of a brutal corporate restructuring to fund AI and cloud infrastructure investments. This massive workforce reduction signals a major shift in legacy tech labor strategies, affecting hiring trends and operational planning for partners and competitors in the enterprise software sector.
